Background:
As a child, Yagura took part in the village's barbaric ritual where Academy students would be forced to kill each other in order to graduate. Following the Three-Tails' revival after being killed in Rin Nohara, the tailed beast was sealed within a young Yagura and he learned to control its powers over the following years. As a jinchūriki, he became known as the third person to achieve full control over a tailed beast. Yagura, being the strongest shinobi in his village at that time, was elected as the Fourth Mizukage. In the anime, he effectively united the various islands of the Land of Water under his leadership.
thumb|left|Yagura being defeated by Itachi.
In the anime, when two members of Akatsuki infiltrated the Land of Water, Yagura joined a team of hunter-nin to apprehend them. The hunter-nin all died during the battle, but Yagura was able to kill Jūzō Biwa by using a version 2 form; Jūzō's partner, Itachi Uchiha, immobilised Yagura with Amaterasu so that he could escape. At some point, Yagura killed Iwabee Yuino's grandfather.
thumb|Yagura being confronted by Akatsuki. 
Around this time, a masked man (who claimed to be Madara Uchiha) took control of Yagura, effectively gaining control over the village. In the anime, this confrontation took place in an encounter with Obito, Pain, and Konan. During his reign, he became widely known for his hand in various crimes such as exploiting money from the weak and his zero-tolerance against any dissent in the village, reinforcing its nickname as {{translation|"Village of the Bloody Mist"|血霧の里|Chigiri no Sato}}. According to one of the Boruto novels, the masked man used his control of Yagura to steal money, information, and other supplies from Kiri for use by Akatsuki. This motivated the tyranny of Bloody Mist, in order to cover up and compensate for the stolen resources. After Kisame Hoshigaki killed Fuguki Suikazan for leaking intel to other villages, Yagura commended Kisame for killing the traitor, before "Madara" revealed that he was manipulating the Mizukage.
His tyrannical reign led many in Kiri to believing he was being manipulated, as well as other villagers wanting him to be executed. Zabuza Momochi was one such individual who came to disagree with Yagura's reign, to which he led a coup d'etat that ended with a failed attempt on his life. Eventually, Ao's Byakugan managed to dispel Tobi's genjutsu, which had a fatal effect on the Fourth Mizukage shortly afterwards.
Personality:
Yagura seems to be very formal, outspoken and composed in an almost diplomatic manner as seen when he welcomed Naruto on behalf of all the jinchūriki and tailed beasts. He does, however, have an exuberant and defensive side as seen by his exasperated outburst at Naruto Uzumaki for thinking he was weaker and younger than him, who had died before experiencing the world as well as ignoring what he was saying. Yagura is also proud of his accomplishment as a Mizukage, claiming to be distinguished in his life. Also, Yagura was very talkative and curious, breaking out of his formality, asking Naruto how it was to kiss Sasuke Uchiha (however, this appeared to be more in the vein of mocking Naruto over curiosity given his annoyed demeanour at the time). 
Having been placed under Tobi's genjutsu during his reign as Mizukage, however, Yagura became a tyrant known to instill fear throughout the village; committing various crimes against them and having absolutely no tolerance for shinobi he deems traitors to the village. He also held grudges against them, such as Jūzō. He was ruthless in battle, refusing mercy to Itachi and Jūzō for challenging him, and was rather direct in battle, as he turned into Version 2 rather quickly to kill his opponents.
Abilities:
thumb|A reincarnated Yagura possessing the Sharingan and Rinnegan.
Yagura was once the most powerful shinobi in his village, which led to his election as the Fourth Mizukage. He also mastered Isobu's power at a young age, becoming a perfect jinchūriki. In the anime, he was able to simultaneously fight Itachi and Jūzō, two members of Akatsuki, killing the latter and nearly killing the former.
Yagura was always seen carrying the Claw Club, a weapon with uneven sized hooks and a green flower on the larger end, implying skills in bōjutsu. As the role of Mizukage is traditionally passed onto the strongest swordsman of the village, it can be inferred he was a greatly skilled kenjutsu user. In the anime, he was shown able to fend off Jūzō Biwa's assault with Kubikiribōchō using his club. After his reincarnation, serving as one of Tobi's Six Paths of Pain, Yagura was seen with both the Sharingan and Rinnegan. Combined with the perceptual and predictive abilities of the Sharingan, and the shared field of vision ability of the Rinnegan, Yagura is able to battle more effectively and precisely. Just like the rest of the Kage, Yagura was capable of using the Summoning Technique, although what he was capable of calling upon is unknown.
[Nature Transformation] thumb|left|Yagura using Water Release Ninjutsu with his weapon.
Yagura was able to incorporate his large, hook-ended club into his Water Release ninjutsu, which allowed him to create water mirrors that can reflect attacks by rotating the "mirror" 90°, causing the reflections to emerge and materialise from the mirror to collide with the mirrored targets. As these reflections use the exact same technique as their counterpart — regardless of its type — they repel all attacks successfully. However, upon contact, the reflections disperse into water. In the anime, he also was proficient in Wind Release, able to swiftly dispel a thick fog with a strong gust.
[Jinchūriki Transformations] {{Main|Jinchūriki Forms}}
thumb|Yagura in his Version 2 form.
As the Three-Tails' jinchūriki, Yagura was granted a significant amount of chakra and stamina from the beast. He was also one of the few people regarded to have achieved full control over their respective beast. He displayed a partial transformation into the Three-Tails, being able to manifest one of its tails. Furthermore, when attacked and immobilised by B's clone, Yagura transformed into his Version 2 form to escape being sealed.
